name: Security Federal Savings Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank headquartered in Indiana with 8 branches across central Indiana, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and investment services.
description_long:
Security Federal Savings Bank is a community bank operating primarily in Indiana with branch locations in Logansport, Kokomo, Delphi, Lafayette, West Lafayette, and West Carmel. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, indicating federal deposit insurance protection up to applicable limits. The institution has been serving local communities and positions itself as deeply rooted in regional economic development.

The bank offers comprehensive consumer and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, Health Savings Accounts (HSAs), and Certificates of Deposit (CDs). On the lending side, they provide mortgage loans for purchase, refinance, and new construction with government programs, home equity loans and HELOCs, personal loans, auto and motorcycle loans, boat and recreation financing, and student loan refinancing. Business customers can access term loans, lines of credit, SBA lending, equipment finance, and agricultural lending. Additional services include digital banking with online and mobile platforms, credit cards (both SFSB-branded and Elan), trust services, investment services, insurance services, and business solutions like payroll, remote deposit capture, and wire originations.

Security Federal distinguishes itself through genuine community investment—the bank commits 10% of earnings to area scholarships, donations, and charitable contributions. Their Peace of Mind Money Market account offers tiered rates up to 1.50% APY with built-in life insurance coverage (up to $25,000) at no extra cost, requiring only a $50 minimum opening deposit. The bank provides IntraFi Network Deposits for customers seeking protection beyond standard FDIC limits. They offer multiple digital banking channels and maintain fee-free ATM access, though specific details on ATM networks were not disclosed in available content.

As a regional community bank, Security Federal is best suited for customers in Indiana seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-making and community reinvestment. However, prospective customers should note that the bank's geographic footprint is limited to Indiana, potentially restricting accessibility for those outside the service area. Interest rates, fee structures, and specific loan terms were not detailed in the available website content, making direct comparison with larger national banks difficult without further investigation. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings, Money Market, HSA, and CD accounts
Mortgage loans (purchase, refinance, new construction, government programs)
Personal, auto, motorcycle, boat, and recreation loans
Business term loans and lines of credit
Trust services (wills, executor, trusts, agency agreements, financial guardianship)
Digital banking (online, mobile app, bill pay, person-to-person transfers, mobile check deposit)
Business solutions (payroll, remote deposit capture, wire originations, invoice processing)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Peace of Mind Money Market account includes up to $25,000 life insurance coverage at no cost with no forms or premiums required
Competitive money market rates up to 1.50% APY with tiered structure and only $50 minimum to open
Commits 10% of earnings to community scholarships, donations, and charitable contributions
8 branch locations across Indiana with drive-thru and ATM-only options for accessibility
Comprehensive services including mortgages, SBA lending, agricultural lending, and trust/investment services
IntraFi Network Deposits available for deposit protection beyond standard FDIC limits
Digital banking platform with mobile app, person-to-person transfers, mobile check deposit, and bill pay
cons: Geographic coverage limited to Indiana only—not accessible for customers outside the state
Specific interest rates, APRs, and fee schedules not transparently displayed on website, requiring direct contact for pricing
Money Market account carries $10 monthly service fee if balance falls below $2,500 minimum maintenance requirement
Limited information available on credit card rewards programs, terms, or competitive positioning versus national card issuers
Small regional institution may have fewer resources and technology capabilities compared to large national banks
best_for: Indiana residents seeking community banking with local decision-making and reinvestment in their area
Customers prioritizing life insurance protection built into savings products without additional premiums
Small business owners and agricultural customers needing personalized lending relationships and SBA/equipment financing
High-net-worth individuals seeking trust services, investment management, and estate planning in a community bank setting
diagnosis: Security Federal Savings Bank is best for Indiana-based consumers and small business owners seeking community banking with genuine local reinvestment and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Indiana and the need to contact the bank directly for current rates, fees, and specific loan terms, as pricing is not transparently published online.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
